memoryArtifacts.headline: Marguerite d'Angleterre (1255-1333)
memoryArtifacts.description: Marguerite d'Angleterre (15 mars 1275 - après 1333) était le dixième enfant et la septième fille du roi Edouard Ier d'Angleterre. Elle épousa à Jean II de Brabant le 8 juillet 1290. Elle fut enterrée …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret of England於1275年9月11日出生在英國英格蘭波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素時，她的父親Edward I King of England36歲，母親Eleanor of Castile Queen of England35歲。她於1290年7月9日與Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT在英國英格蘭米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1318年在比利時布拉班特享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret of England于1275年9月11日出生在英国英格兰波克夏溫莎女爵 卡素时，她的父亲Edward I King of England36岁，母亲Eleanor of Castile Queen of England35岁。她于1290年7月9日与Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT在英国英格兰米德萊可維斯特明斯特維斯特明斯特 艾比结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1318年在比利时布拉班特享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret of England was born on 11 September 1275, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edward I King of England, was 36 and her mother, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, was 35. She married Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT on 9 July 1290, in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1318, in Brabant, Belgium, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret of England est née le 11 septembre 1275, à Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edward I King of England, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, avait 35 ans. Elle a épousé Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT le 9 juillet 1290, à Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1318, à Brabant, Belgique, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret of England am 11. September 1275 in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Edward I King of England 36 und ihre Mutter Eleanor of Castile Queen of England 35. Sie hat am 9. Juli 1290 in Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1318 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Brabant, Belgien.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret of Englandが1275年9月11日にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルで生まれた時，父Edward I King of Englandは36歳，母Eleanor of Castile Queen of Englandは35歳であった。1290年7月9日にイギリスイギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスターウエストミンスター アビーでJean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANTと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1318年にベルギーブラバントで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret of England(이)가 1275년 9월 11일 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward I King of England(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor of Castile Queen of England(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 1290년 7월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 웨스트민스터 애브브이에서 Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1318년 벨기에 브라반트에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret of England nasceu em 11 de setembro de 1275, em Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Edward I King of England, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, tinha 35 anos. Ela casou-se com Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT em 9 de julho de 1290, em Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1318, em Brabante, Bélgica, com 43 anos.

ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, London,: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlese: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret of England родилась 11 сентября 1275 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Edward I King of England было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, 35. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT 9 июля 1290 г. в местности вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1318 г. в местности брабант, Бельгия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlese: Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret of England nació el 11 de septiembre de 1275, en Windsor Castle, Berkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Edward I King of England, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Eleanor of Castile Queen of England, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT el 9 de julio de 1290, en Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1318, en Brabante, Bélgica, a la edad de 43 años.
